title: Peloton Q4 2023 Earnings Analysis
text: Peloton Q4 2023 Earnings Analysis. Financial Performance: Peloton reported mixed results for Q4 and fiscal 2023. Revenue exceeded expectations at $642.1 million versus $639 million projected, though the company posted a wider-than-expected loss of 68 cents per share compared to 38 cents anticipated. The net loss improved significantly to $241.8 million from $1.26 billion the prior year. A notable achievement was reaching cash flow positivity in the quarter when excluding the DISH settlement, though McCarthy cautioned this wouldn't continue through the next two quarters due to seasonality and inventory costs. Key Product Updates: The Tread+ return generated significant attention. The company opened pre-orders for the holiday season at $6,000, drawing from 10,000 existing units. They've received nearly 17,000 requests from current owners seeking the newly approved rear guard retrofit. The seat post recall proved costly, with 750,000 total requests and a $40 million accrual for completion by September. Leadership estimates 15,000-20,000 membership pauses stem from this issue. Strategic Initiatives: Peloton launched Peloton for Business, partnered with NCAA Division 1 schools including Michigan, and announced upcoming limited-edition bike colors marking a departure from the any color as long as it's black approach that characterized the company's first decade.
